928	\section{Lax-monoidal global sections}
929	\label{sec:sgr_lax_sections}
930	
931	The graded multiplication is supplied by the lax-monoidal structure of the
932	global-sections functor \(\Gamma(X, -) : \ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X) \to
933	\mathrm{ModuleCat}(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X))\). It is only \emph{lax} because
934	global sections do not commute with sheafification: a global section of a
935	sheafified tensor product is not in general a finite sum of elementary tensors of
936	global sections.
937	
938	\begin{definition}\leanok
939	[Section multiplication]
940	  \label{def:sectionMul}
941	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sectionsMul}
942	  \uses{def:sheafTensorObj, lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
943	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
944	  Let \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\) be sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The
945	  \emph{section multiplication} is the \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\)-bilinear map
946	  \[
947	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_{\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)}
948	      \Gamma(X, \mathcal{G})
949	    \;\longrightarrow\;
950	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})
951	  \]
952	  defined as follows. A pair of global sections \((\sigma, \tau)\) determines,
953	  by the objectwise formula of \cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib} at the
954	  top open, the elementary tensor \(\sigma \otimes \tau \in
955	  (\mathcal{F} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})(X)\), a global section of
956	  the tensor product presheaf. Composing with the global-sections component of
957	  the sheafification unit
958	  \(\eta : \mathcal{F} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G} \to
959	  (\mathcal{F} \otimes_{p,\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})^{\#} =
960	  \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G}\)
961	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib},
962	  \cref{def:sheafTensorObj}) yields a global section
963	  \(\sigma \cdot \tau \in
964	  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{G})\). Bilinearity over
965	  \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\) is inherited from bilinearity of the elementary
966	  tensor and \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\)-linearity of \(\eta\).
967	\end{definition}
968	
969	\begin{lemma}[Coherence of the section multiplication]
970	  \label{lem:sectionMul_coherent}
971	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sectionsMul_assoc_unit}
972	  \uses{def:sectionMul, lem:moduleUnit_mathlib, lem:sheafTensorPow_add}
973	  The section multiplications (\cref{def:sectionMul}) are associative and unital
974	  in the following sense. For sheaves \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}, \mathcal{H}\)
975	  the two composites
976	  \[
977	    \Gamma(\mathcal{F}) \otimes \Gamma(\mathcal{G}) \otimes \Gamma(\mathcal{H})
978	    \longrightarrow
979	    \Gamma\bigl((\mathcal{F} \otimes \mathcal{G}) \otimes \mathcal{H}\bigr)
980	    \cong
981	    \Gamma\bigl(\mathcal{F} \otimes (\mathcal{G} \otimes \mathcal{H})\bigr)
982	  \]
983	  agree after transport along the associator of \cref{def:sheafTensorObj}; and
984	  the unit element \(1 \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\)
985	  (\cref{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}) acts as a two-sided identity, i.e.\ the section
986	  multiplication by \(1\) is, after the unitor isomorphism
987	  \(\mathcal{O}_X \otimes \mathcal{F} \cong \mathcal{F}\), the identity on
988	  \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\).
989	\end{lemma}
990	
991	\begin{proof}
992	  \uses{def:sectionMul, lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib,
993	    lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}
994	  Both statements reduce to the corresponding identities at the level of the
995	  tensor product presheaf, where by \cref{lem:presheafModule_monoidal_mathlib}
996	  evaluation at the top open is strictly monoidal, so the associator and unitors
997	  act on elementary tensors by the usual formulas
998	  \((\sigma \otimes \tau) \otimes \upsilon \mapsto
999	  \sigma \otimes (\tau \otimes \upsilon)\) and
1000	  \(1 \otimes \sigma \mapsto \sigma\). The sheafification unit \(\eta\) is a
1001	  natural transformation, so it commutes with the associator and unitor
1002	  (\cref{lem:presheafModule_sheafification_mathlib}); applying \(\Gamma(X,-)\)
1003	  to the naturality squares transports these presheaf-level identities to the
1004	  section multiplications.
1005	\end{proof}

1013	abelian groups indexed by \(\mathbb{N}\) together with multiplication maps
1014	landing in the index-sum component, not submodules of a pre-existing ring.

1016	\begin{lemma}[External-direct-sum graded commutative semiring]
